skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>clawhub</name>
    <description>Use the ClawHub CLI to search, install, update, and publish agent skills from clawhub.com. Use when you need to fetch new skills on the fly, sync installed skills to latest or a specific version, or publish new/updated skill folders with the npm-installed clawhub CLI.</description>
    <location>~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/clawhub/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>healthcheck</name>
    <description>Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).</description>
    <location>~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>node-connect</name>
    <description>Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.</description>
    <location>~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/node-connect/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>skill-creator</name>
    <description>Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".</description>
    <location>~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/skill-creator/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>taskflow</name>
    <description>Use when work should span one or more detached tasks but still behave like one job with a single owner context. TaskFlow is the durable flow substrate under authoring layers like Lobster, ACPX, plugins, or plain code. Keep conditional logic in the caller; use TaskFlow for flow identity, child-task linkage, waiting state, revision-checked mutations, and user-facing emergence.</description>
    <location>~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/taskflow/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>taskflow-inbox-triage</name>
    <description>Example TaskFlow authoring pattern for inbox triage. Use when messages need different treatment based on intent, with some routes notifying immediately, some waiting on outside answers, and others rolling into a later summary.</description>
    <location>~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/taskflow-inbox-triage/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>weather</name>
    <description>Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.</description>
    <location>~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/weather/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
</available_skills>

compactionCheckpoints.summary:
## Goal
- Julius is setting up and using Kara (OpenClaw agent) to build a personal knowledge base
- Tasks include: summarizing and ingesting articles/repos into `raw/`, configuring Agent-Reach tools on VPS, general information queries

## Constraints & Preferences
- Vietnamese language, "em" / "anh" pronouns
- Emoji default: 👌
- Summaries in Vietnamese, minimize English terminology
- Brief communication style — no bullet points when unnecessary
- Report after task completion only (not progress reports)
- Ask one question at a time
- If uncertain → ask Julius before acting
- Agent-Reach currently installed on VPS (julius@julius-vps)

## Progress
### Done
- [x] Kara identity established (name, personality, SOUL.md, IDENTITY.md, USER.md)
- [x] SOUL.md updated with communication preferences (Vietnamese, limit English terms, default emoji 👌)
- [x] AGENTS.md updated: added `#keyboard`, `#website-hay` tags to standard tags list
- [x] Gateway pairing issue resolved (restart via `npx openclaw gateway restart`)
- [x] Ingested 11 items into raw/:

**Repos (raw/repos/):**
- 2026-04-20_juliuss1907_firecrawl.md
- 2026-04-20_juliuss1907_agent-reach.md
- 2026-04-20_juliuss1907_camoufox.md
- 2026-04-20_juliuss1907_meilisearch.md
- 2026-04-20_juliuss1907_openclaw-studio.md

**Articles (raw/articles/):**
- 2026-04-20_sheets-works-listening-museum.md (#keyboard)
- 2026-04-20_carcheck-top.md (#website-hay)
- 2026-04-20_zengineer-kelp-dao-292m-hack.md (#crypto, #web3, #security)
- 2026-04-20_layerzero-kelpdao-incident-statement.md (#crypto, #web3, #security)
- 2026-04-20_david-coffee-mcp-vs-skills.md (#ai, #llm, #coding)
- 2026-04-20_sciencealert-gut-transit-time.md (#health, #research)

- [x] Agent-Reach installed on VPS (10/16 channels active: GitHub, YouTube, Reddit, WeChat, V2EX, RSS, Exa Search, Web via Jina, Twitter/X, Bilibili)
- [x] Twitter cookies configured via `agent-reach configure twitter-cookies`
- [x] Gas price, gold, Bitcoin, world news provided (April 20, 2026)
- [x] Read Twitter post via `twitter tweet 2046202795163103435` (summary provided)

### In Progress
- None currently — awaiting Julius's next task

### Blocked
- (none)

## Key Decisions
- **OpenClaw gateway pairing**: Resolved by running `npx openclaw gateway restart`
- **Agent-Reach installation**: Used pipx approach (`sudo apt install pipx` → `pipx install ...`)
- **Twitter cookies**: Stored in config via `agent-reach configure twitter-cookies` (not passed to Kara due to security)
- **#keyboard tag**: Added to standard tags (no fit in existing list for mechanical keyboard content)
- **#website-hay tag**: Added to standard tags (Julius's custom tag for noteworthy sites)

## Next Steps
1. Wait for Julius's next task (summarize/ingest article, use Agent-Reach tools, etc.)
2. Agent-Reach compile will run automatically at 08:00 tomorrow morning
3. If Julius wants more Agent-Reach channels (小红书, 微博, 雪球, etc.) → provide setup instructions

## Critical Context
- **Workspace**: `/home/julius/knowledge-base/knowledge-base-agent/`
- **OpenClaw config**: `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json`
- **Agent-Reach venv**: `~/.agent-reach-venv/bin/activate` (if needed)
- **Agent-Reach skill**: `/home/julius/.agents/skills/agent-reach`
- **Standard tags**: #ai, #llm, #crypto, #web3, #business, #marketing, #content, #productivity, #health, #coding, #research, #finance, #mindset, #keyboard, #website-hay
- **Active providers**: MiniMax-M2.7 (chat), Google/Gemini (web search)
- **Agent-Reach channels (10/16 active)**: Web (Jina Reader), YouTube, Reddit (rdt-cli, logged in as Key_Ad1316), WeChat, V2EX, RSS, Exa Search, Twitter/X (cookies configured), Bilibili, GitHub (gh CLI installed)
- **Cron jobs**: Currently empty (none configured yet)

- Assistant extracted the article content and wrote it to `/home/julius/knowledge-base/knowledge-base-agent/raw/articles/2026-04-20_trithucvn-may-say-toc.md`
- Content includes a structured summary with acupressure points by body location, usage precautions, and tagged with `#health`
- Total 1603 bytes written

## Context for Suffix
The assistant confirmed to Julius that the article was saved successfully with the `#health` tag.

skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>agent-reach</name>
    <description>Give your AI agent eyes to see the entire internet. Search and read 17 platforms: Twitter/X, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, XiaoHongShu, Douyin, Weibo, WeChat Articles, Xiaoyuzhou Podcast, LinkedIn, V2EX, Xueqiu, RSS, Exa web search, and any web page. Zero config for 8 channels. Use when the user asks to search, read, or interact on any supported platform, shares a URL, or asks to search the web. Triggers: "search twitter", "search xiaohongshu", "watch this video", "search the web", "look this up", "research", "youtube transcript", "search reddit", "read this link", "bilibili", "douyin video", "wechat article", "wechat official account", "weibo", "V2EX", "xiaoyuzhou", "podcast", "xueqiu", "stock quote", "install agent reach".</description>
    <location>~/.agents/skills/agent-reach/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>healthcheck</name>
    <description>Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).</description>
    <location>~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/healthcheck/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>nano-pdf</name>
    <description>Edit PDFs with natural-language instructions using the nano-pdf CLI.</description>
    <location>~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/nano-pdf/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>node-connect</name>
    <description>Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.</description>
    <location>~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/node-connect/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>skill-creator</name>
    <description>Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".</description>
    <location>~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/skill-creator/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>taskflow</name>
    <description>Use when work should span one or more detached tasks but still behave like one job with a single owner context. TaskFlow is the durable flow substrate under authoring layers like Lobster, ACPX, plugins, or plain code. Keep conditional logic in the caller; use TaskFlow for flow identity, child-task linkage, waiting state, revision-checked mutations, and user-facing emergence.</description>
    <location>~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/taskflow/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>taskflow-inbox-triage</name>
    <description>Example TaskFlow authoring pattern for inbox triage. Use when messages need different treatment based on intent, with some routes notifying immediately, some waiting on outside answers, and others rolling into a later summary.</description>
    <location>~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/taskflow-inbox-triage/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>weather</name>
    <description>Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.</description>
    <location>~/.nvm/versions/node/v24.15.0/lib/node_modules/openclaw/skills/weather/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
</available_skills>

TaskFlow is the durable flow substrate under authoring layers like Lobster, ACPX, plugins, or plain code. Keep conditional logic in the caller; use TaskFlow for flow identity, child-task linkage, waiting state, revision-checked mutations, and user-facing emergence.

resolvedSkills.name: taskflow-inbox-triage
resolvedSkills.description: Example TaskFlow authoring pattern for inbox triage. Use when messages need different treatment based on intent, with some routes notifying immediately, some waiting on outside answers, and others rolling into a later summary.

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

skillsSnapshot.prompt:
The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

resolvedSkills.name: taskflow
resolvedSkills.description: Use when work should span one or more detached tasks but still behave like one job with a single owner context. TaskFlow is the durable flow substrate under authoring layers like Lobster, ACPX, plugins, or plain code. Keep conditional logic in the caller; use TaskFlow for flow identity, child-task linkage, waiting state, revision-checked mutations, and user-facing emergence.

Zero config for 8 channels. Use when the user asks to search, read, or interact on any supported platform, shares a URL, or asks to search the web. Triggers: "search twitter", "search xiaohongshu", "watch this video", "search the web", "look this up", "research", "youtube transcript", "search reddit", "read this link", "bilibili", "douyin video", "wechat article", "wechat official account", "weibo", "V2EX", "xiaoyuzhou", "podcast", "xueqiu", "stock quote", "install agent reach".

resolvedSkills.name: gh-issues
resolvedSkills.description: Fetch GitHub issues, spawn sub-agents to implement fixes and open PRs, then monitor and address PR review comments. Usage: /gh-issues [owner/repo] [--label bug] [--limit 5] [--milestone v1.0] [--assignee @me] [--fork user/repo] [--watch] [--interval 5] [--reviews-only] [--cron] [--dry-run] [--model glm-5] [--notify-channel -1002381931352]

resolvedSkills.name: github
resolvedSkills.description: GitHub operations via `gh` CLI: issues, PRs, CI runs, code review, API queries. Use when: (1) checking PR status or CI, (2) creating/commenting on issues, (3) listing/filtering PRs or issues, (4) viewing run logs. NOT for: complex web UI interactions requiring manual browser flows (use browser tooling when available), bulk operations across many repos (script with gh api), or when gh auth is not configured.

resolvedSkills.name: healthcheck
resolvedSkills.description: Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).

resolvedSkills.name: nano-pdf
resolvedSkills.description: Edit PDFs with natural-language instructions using the nano-pdf CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: node-connect
resolvedSkills.description: Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

resolvedSkills.name: taskflow
resolvedSkills.description: Use when work should span one or more detached tasks but still behave like one job with a single owner context. TaskFlow is the durable flow substrate under authoring layers like Lobster, ACPX, plugins, or plain code. Keep conditional logic in the caller; use TaskFlow for flow identity, child-task linkage, waiting state, revision-checked mutations, and user-facing emergence.

resolvedSkills.name: taskflow-inbox-triage
resolvedSkills.description: Example TaskFlow authoring pattern for inbox triage. Use when messages need different treatment based on intent, with some routes notifying immediately, some waiting on outside answers, and others rolling into a later summary.

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

resolvedSkills.name: taskflow
resolvedSkills.description: Use when work should span one or more detached tasks but still behave like one job with a single owner context. TaskFlow is the durable flow substrate under authoring layers like Lobster, ACPX, plugins, or plain code. Keep conditional logic in the caller; use TaskFlow for flow identity, child-task linkage, waiting state, revision-checked mutations, and user-facing emergence.

Zero config for 8 channels. Use when the user asks to search, read, or interact on any supported platform, shares a URL, or asks to search the web. Triggers: "search twitter", "search xiaohongshu", "watch this video", "search the web", "look this up", "research", "youtube transcript", "search reddit", "read this link", "bilibili", "douyin video", "wechat article", "wechat official account", "weibo", "V2EX", "xiaoyuzhou", "podcast", "xueqiu", "stock quote", "install agent reach".

resolvedSkills.name: gh-issues
resolvedSkills.description: Fetch GitHub issues, spawn sub-agents to implement fixes and open PRs, then monitor and address PR review comments. Usage: /gh-issues [owner/repo] [--label bug] [--limit 5] [--milestone v1.0] [--assignee @me] [--fork user/repo] [--watch] [--interval 5] [--reviews-only] [--cron] [--dry-run] [--model glm-5] [--notify-channel -1002381931352]

resolvedSkills.name: github
resolvedSkills.description: GitHub operations via `gh` CLI: issues, PRs, CI runs, code review, API queries. Use when: (1) checking PR status or CI, (2) creating/commenting on issues, (3) listing/filtering PRs or issues, (4) viewing run logs. NOT for: complex web UI interactions requiring manual browser flows (use browser tooling when available), bulk operations across many repos (script with gh api), or when gh auth is not configured.

resolvedSkills.name: healthcheck
resolvedSkills.description: Host security hardening and risk-tolerance configuration for OpenClaw deployments. Use when a user asks for security audits, firewall/SSH/update hardening, risk posture, exposure review, OpenClaw cron scheduling for periodic checks, or version status checks on a machine running OpenClaw (laptop, workstation, Pi, VPS).

resolvedSkills.name: nano-pdf
resolvedSkills.description: Edit PDFs with natural-language instructions using the nano-pdf CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: node-connect
resolvedSkills.description: Diagnose OpenClaw node connection and pairing failures for Android, iOS, and macOS companion apps. Use when QR/setup code/manual connect fails, local Wi-Fi works but VPS/tailnet does not, or errors mention pairing required, unauthorized, bootstrap token invalid or expired, gateway.bind, gateway.remote.url, Tailscale, or plugins.entries.device-pair.config.publicUrl.

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create, edit, improve, or audit AgentSkills. Use when creating a new skill from scratch or when asked to improve, review, audit, tidy up, or clean up an existing skill or SKILL.md file. Also use when editing or restructuring a skill directory (moving files to references/ or scripts/, removing stale content, validating against the AgentSkills spec). Triggers on phrases like "create a skill", "author a skill", "tidy up a skill", "improve this skill", "review the skill", "clean up the skill", "audit the skill".

resolvedSkills.name: taskflow
resolvedSkills.description: Use when work should span one or more detached tasks but still behave like one job with a single owner context. TaskFlow is the durable flow substrate under authoring layers like Lobster, ACPX, plugins, or plain code. Keep conditional logic in the caller; use TaskFlow for flow identity, child-task linkage, waiting state, revision-checked mutations, and user-facing emergence.

resolvedSkills.name: taskflow-inbox-triage
resolvedSkills.description: Example TaskFlow authoring pattern for inbox triage. Use when messages need different treatment based on intent, with some routes notifying immediately, some waiting on outside answers, and others rolling into a later summary.

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.
